articles.title: Daniel Moreno-Gama is facing federal charges for attacking Sam Altman’s home and OpenAI’s HQ
articles.summary: Daniel Moreno-Gama faces federal charges after traveling from Texas to California and attacking Sam Altman's home with a Molotov cocktail and attempting to break into OpenAI headquarters.

articles.title: AI influencers are ‘everywhere’ at Coachella
articles.summary: AI-generated influencers are proliferating at Coachella, creating fake attendance posts alongside real influencers. These virtual personas use generative AI tools to produce polished, staged content for social media.

articles.title: Read OpenAI’s latest internal memo about beating the competition — including Anthropic
articles.summary: OpenAI's CRO sent an internal memo emphasizing the need to lock in users and expand enterprise business while building competitive advantages against rivals like Anthropic.

articles.title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
articles.summary: OpenAI acquired AI personal finance startup Hiro, signaling plans to integrate financial planning capabilities into ChatGPT.

articles.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
articles.summary: Microsoft is developing an enterprise-focused agent similar to OpenClaw, featuring enhanced security controls for safer deployment in business environments.

articles.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
articles.summary: Stanford's AI Index reveals a widening gap between AI experts and the public, with growing public anxiety about job displacement, healthcare, and economic impacts.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS. The funding capitalizes on developer demand for simpler, more cost-effective alternatives to legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but free alternative Goose offers similar functionality as an open-source tool running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring encoded AI tokens and a coding challenge, attracting thousands of candidates and successfully recruiting engineers.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated approximately 30,000 VMware customers due to customer dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware acquisition and strategy.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure operations by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, water treatment centers, and oil refineries. Six US government agencies issued an urgent warning about the advanced persistent threat group.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence agency GRU hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: Why opinion on AI is so divided
articles.summary: Stanford's AI Index report reveals striking statistics about AI development trends, including the US's increased focus on AI advancement. The report provides data-backed insights into the rapidly evolving AI industry landscape.

articles.title: Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.
articles.summary: Stanford's 2026 AI Index reports that top AI models continue improving despite predictions of slowdown. AI adoption outpaces historical tech booms, but rapid development strains benchmarks, policies, and the job market.

articles.title: The Download: how humans make decisions, and Moderna’s “vaccine” word games
articles.summary: Researcher Uri Maoz explores the philosophical question of whether humans truly make choices or if decisions are predetermined. He investigates how desires and beliefs translate into actions through computational neuroscience.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive virtual worlds on consumer GPUs. This advancement makes sophisticated 3D environment generation more accessible to everyday developers.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling cross-modal search and ranking across text, images, and other data types simultaneously.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve on-the-job through continuous training. The framework allows agents to adapt and enhance their capabilities during real-world task execution.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases open-source toolkit for future farming technologies, enabling developers to build agricultural solutions using AI and advanced tools.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to balance cost and latency tradeoffs for users.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI video creation and editing features powered by Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect and monitor Brazil's forests more effectively.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models yet, specifically designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live enhances voice interactions with improved precision and lower latency, delivering more natural and fluid audio AI conversations.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI's potential for harmful manipulation in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect users from risks.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency, while Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family. The episode discusses these developments and other major AI news from the week.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances real-time photorealism in games up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate realistic details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing a key challenge in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster reasoning reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a tradeoff between speed and generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them emerge as the true competitive advantage. The Anthropic Claude Code leak demonstrates that superior engineering infrastructure, not just base models, creates lasting market differentiation.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Google ADK Multi-Agent Pipeline Tutorial: Data Loading, Statistical Testing, Visualization, and Report Generation in Python
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a multi-agent data analysis pipeline using Google ADK in Python. Covers data loading, statistical testing, visualization, and report generation through specialized agents coordinated by a master analyst.

articles.title: Google AI Research Proposes Vantage: An LLM-Based Protocol for Measuring Collaboration, Creativity, and Critical Thinking
articles.summary: Google Research introduces Vantage, an LLM-based protocol using orchestrated language models to measure collaboration, creativity, and critical thinking in students. It simulates authentic group interactions and scores results with accuracy comparable to human expert raters.

articles.title: A Step-by-Step Coding Tutorial on NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo: Darcy Flow, FNOs, PINNs, Surrogate Models, and Inference Benchmarking
articles.summary: NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo tutorial implements physics-informed machine learning on Colab, covering Fourier Neural Operators, PINNs, and surrogate models for 2D Darcy Flow problems with inference benchmarking.

articles.title: I carried this Bluetooth tracker card in my backpack for a week - and it withstood my clumsiness
articles.summary: The UAG Metropolis tracker serves as a durable AirTag alternative, withstanding a week of rough handling and bending without damage.

articles.title: I tested ChatGPT Plus vs. Gemini Pro to see which is better - and if it's worth switching
articles.summary: A comparison test of ChatGPT Plus and Gemini Pro across 10 identical tasks reveals which premium AI service performs better, helping users decide if switching is worthwhile.

articles.title: I followed the 'Plus 5' rule for wireless charging, and it fixed my iPhone's charge speeds
articles.summary: Following the 'Plus 5' rule can optimize iPhone wireless charging speeds. The article advises checking accessories to ensure you're achieving maximum charging performance.

articles.title: How to Implement Tool Calling with Gemma 4 and Python
articles.summary: Learn how to implement tool calling with Gemma 4 using Python. This guide covers integrating function calls into the open-weights model for enhanced capabilities.

articles.title: Structured Outputs vs. Function Calling: Which Should Your Agent Use?
articles.summary: The article explores the differences between structured outputs and function calling for AI agents, examining how language models handle text-based interactions and which approach works best.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: This article explores a deterministic 3-tiered graph-RAG system that goes beyond traditional vector search. It presents methods for building more structured and reliable retrieval-augmented generation systems using graph-based approaches.

articles.title: How to Apply Claude Code to Non-technical Tasks
articles.summary: Learn how to apply Claude's coding agents to handle non-technical tasks across your computer, expanding the utility of AI coding tools beyond traditional programming work.

articles.title: Your Model Isn’t Done: Understanding and Fixing Model Drift
articles.summary: Production models degrade over time due to data drift. The article explains how to detect and fix model drift before it damages user trust.

articles.title: Range Over Depth: A Reflection on the Role of the Data Generalist
articles.summary: The article examines how data generalists' roles have evolved over the past five years, emphasizing breadth of skills over specialized depth in modern data teams.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs bringing robots into the physical world across industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a game about grit and faith, now streams on GeForce NOW. Players can experience the mythic narrative on any device through cloud gaming.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: How to build effective reward functions with AWS Lambda for Amazon Nova model customization
articles.summary: AWS Lambda enables cost-effective reward functions for Amazon Nova model customization through serverless architecture. Reinforcement fine-tuning allows iterative feedback to teach models desired behaviors.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Users should test models before migrating applications to ensure performance and compatibility.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, helps enterprises manage agent sprawl by enabling discovery, sharing, and reuse of agents and tools across organizations while providing visibility and governance controls.

articles.title: MiniMax M2.7 Advances Scalable Agentic Workflows on NVIDIA Platforms for Complex AI Applications
articles.summary: MiniMax M2.7 enhances the M2.5 model with improved capabilities for agentic workflows and complex AI applications in reasoning, research, and engineering tasks. The open-weights model is now available through NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster manager used by 65% of TOP500 systems, enables large-scale GPU workload scheduling on Kubernetes while preserving existing job scripts and policies.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP enables reducing LLM checkpoint costs with minimal Python code. The tool compresses massive model snapshots, significantly lowering storage expenses for large-scale training.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study explores how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertising across various platforms, examining generational attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional experience leveraged their unique perspective to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse backgrounds can drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understand model behavior, advancing toward safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven design methodology for imaging systems that optimizes encoder performance using noisy measurements and noise models to improve image quality and measurement distinction.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks.
